learning.concept: Instructions are step-by-step actions that we follow in our daily lives.
learning.explanation: Every task we perform follows an exact sequence to reach a goal. Computers require the same level of sequence to function correctly.

analogy.title: Making Food
analogy.body:
Making Maggi noodles follows a program:

1. Boil water
2. Add noodles
3. Add masala
4. Cook for 2 minutes

👉 If you skip a step, the results will not be what you expected!

practice.q: Write steps to brush teeth like a program.
practice.a: 1. Pick up brush | 2. Apply toothpaste | 3. Brush teeth | 4. Rinse with water.

practice.q: What happens if steps are in the wrong order?
practice.a: You will get an error or a wrong result, just like in code.

practice.q: Is skipping a step okay in coding?
practice.a: No, every instruction is vital to the final output.
